questions: Resize policy: when the user drags a group's outer edge, should child clip durations scale uniformly (group rescales as a whole), or should only the edge child absorb the delta (others stay put)? The brief's requirement #3 favors relative-positioning cohesion but doesn't specify the scaling rule. I'm assuming uniform scaling.

Interior boundary resize: I propose removing it entirely (the brief says 'likely it should not be'). Confirm this is acceptable — the recent auto-shift megaplan will be partially reverted for group members only (non-group clips keep auto-shift).

Group-level resize handles: should they render as part of the group bounding-box overlay in TimelineCanvas (my plan), or stay as the first/last child clip's handles with special routing? The overlay approach is cleaner but slightly more UI code.

Schema: I plan an additive migration (new `start` + `children` fields, no version bump, legacy configs migrated in-memory on load and re-serialized on next save). Acceptable, or do you want an explicit schema version field on TimelineConfig?

Sync vs. in-progress drag: the brief asks how this interacts. My plan guards sync by checking `useDragCoordinator` for an active drag/resize and deferring. Confirm this guard already exists or should be added.

success_criteria.criterion: `PinnedShotGroup` type gains `start: number` and `children: { clipId, offset, duration }[]` fields; existing fields preserved
Single `projectPinnedShotGroups(config)` function is the sole writer of member clip `start`/`end`/`track`, called from `assembleTimelineData` and `rowsToConfig`
Legacy configs without `start`/`children` are migrated in-memory on load with start = min(clip.start) and children built from clip positions
Drag of any clip inside a pinned group is represented as a single `GroupDragEntry` (no per-clip deferred expansion)
Resize of a group's outer left/right edge goes through `resizeGroupEdge`; resize of an interior boundary between group members is disabled/rejected
`positionedShotGroups` in TimelineCanvas renders group bounding box directly from `group.start` + last child's extent with no in-array-order contiguity check
New property test verifies cohesion-by-construction: after any sequence of moveGroup/resizeGroupEdge/replaceGroupChildren, children are contiguous and spatially ordered
All existing `src/tools/video-editor/` tests pass (with updates where the shape changed); the pre-existing TimelineCanvas `data-shot-group-kind="pinned"` failure is unchanged
Group `start` is clamped to ≥ 0 in every drag and resize path (via `moveGroup`/`findBestGroupStart`)
Remotion preview renders the same output for an unchanged timeline (flat `clips[]` semantics unchanged)

Now-dead code removed: bespoke `getShotGroupAdjacent` pairing, deferred-expansion fields in useClipDrag, collective-extent calc in resolveGroupOverlaps, interior auto-shift in useClipResize for group members
Diff is concentrated in `src/tools/video-editor/` with no unrelated cleanups bundled in
Manual smoke test of the flows listed in Hard Requirement #2 passes
Attempting to break a pinned group via any combination of drag/resize/snap/multi-select/cross-track drag is impossible; only Deconstruct/Delete break it

assumptions: Uniform-scale resize policy for group outer edges; interior-boundary resize is removed for group members.
The existing `useDragCoordinator` exposes or can cheaply expose an 'is dragging/resizing' flag that `usePinnedGroupSync` can check before applying updates.
`TimelineConfig.clips[]` remains the Remotion source of truth; member clip `start`/`end`/`track` are overwritten on every projection pass so Remotion never sees stale positions.
Additive, backward-compatible in-memory migration (no version bump) is acceptable since the on-disk config is a per-project JSON blob.

Selection model stays as-is: single click selects the inner clip; the asset-state-only context menu already prevents breaking the group from the clip level. Cohesion-by-construction in the write path is what actually enforces the invariant.
The failing `data-shot-group-kind="pinned"` test in TimelineCanvas.test.tsx is pre-existing and left alone per the brief.
`findBestGroupStart` is kept as the single authority for t≥0 clamping and is called from `moveGroup`; per-clip clamps on group members are removed.
No changes to Remotion compositions are needed; verification is a read-only check that compositions still receive a flat `clips[]` with the same semantics.
structure_warnings: Each step section should reference at least one file in backticks.
